reforms.description: The Australian government has significantly increased funding for research in higher education institutions, tripling it from A$ 480 million in 1983/84 to an estimated A$ 1.5 billion in 1995/96. Additionally, a new R&D funding programme called START has been introduced, allocating A$ 340 million over four years in competitive grants to support research and development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D is expected to enhance research capabilities and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding programme has been successfully introduced and is currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The significant increase in funding represents a structural change in public investment in R&D.
reforms.source_quote: Government funding for research in higher education institutions tripled from A$ 480 million in 1983/84 to an estimated A$ 1.5 billion in 1995/96.

reforms.description: The introduction of a 150 per cent R&D tax concession has been a key feature of Australia's technology policy, significantly stimulating business sector R&D spending. The government continues to analyze the cost/benefit of technology-related programmes and monitor the effects of changes to the R&D tax concession.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The R&D tax concession incentivizes private sector investment in R&D, which is crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D tax concession has been in effect and is actively stimulating business R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: The R&D tax concession represents a significant reform in the innovation support system, encouraging private sector R&D.
reforms.source_quote: Continue the cost/benefit analysis of technology-related programmes and monitor the effects of changes to the R&D tax concession on business research spending.

reforms.description: The Prime Minister delivered a statement entitled Innovate Australia, announcing a range of measures designed to underpin productivity growth and the creation of new industries and jobs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance productivity and job creation, which are essential for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been officially announced but not yet legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The announcement of a comprehensive initiative represents a significant step towards enhancing innovation in Australia.
reforms.source_quote: The Prime Minister delivered a statement entitled Innovate Australia announcing a range of measures designed to underpin productivity growth and the creation of new industries and jobs.

reforms.description: The Australian government intends to enhance the supply of skilled workers through various reforms, including the removal of obstacles to work-based vocational education and training (VET), the introduction of the National Literacy and Numeracy Strategy, and the implementation of 'user choice' in apprenticeship and traineeship training.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing human capital through education and training is vital for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented and are currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes aimed at improving the skills and training of the workforce.
reforms.source_quote: It funded pilot projects for 'user choice' in apprenticeship and traineeship training which is to be the basis of the provision of training for all apprenticeships and traineeships from the beginning of 1998.
